vulnerabilities.description: Supplying any 'data' cookie (e.g., 'data=invalid') returns 200 with the authenticated notes page (Welcome header and Add Note form). Without the cookie, /index.php responds 302 to /sign-in.php. Presence of the cookie is treated as authentication without validating contents.

After sign-in, the server sets a 'data' cookie containing a base64-encoded PHP serialized object (class UserProfile). Example decoded value: O:11:"UserProfile":2:{s:6:"userid";i:2;s:8:"username";s:...}. Because the server trusts and unserializes client-controlled data, this is likely exploitable for PHP object injection leading to privilege escalation or RCE if a gadget chain exists.
Both 'PHPSESSID' and 'data' cookies are set without HttpOnly or Secure attributes, increasing risk of session theft via XSS and interception over plaintext HTTP.

The Add Note form posts directly to /index.php without any anti-CSRF token in the HTML. A POST creates/updates content successfully, indicating no CSRF protections and enabling cross-site request forgery.
